parts.text:
PATIENT AGE: 11 years → preventive E/M bracket: 99393
## CLAIMS CODING SUMMARY
**Patient:** Alex Mercer | **DOB:** 2013-02-14 | **Payer:** commercial
**Encounter Date:** 2024-11-15
**Coded by:** Claims AI Agent | **Status:** READY TO SUBMIT

### Billable Codes
| CPT | Description | Modifier | Status | Evidence |
|-----|-------------|----------|--------|----------|
| 99215 | Office or other outpatient visit for the evaluation and management of an established patient, typically 40 minutes were spent face-to-face with the patient and/or family. | 25 | Covered | "TOTAL ENCOUNTER TIME: 75 minutes (including pre/post encounter work, chart review, external record review, imaging interpretation, care coordination) Medical decision making complexity: HIGH — 99215" |
| 71046 | Radiologic examination, chest; 2 views | 26 | Covered | "Chest X-ray 2 views (71046) — ordered and preliminary interpretation performed" |
| 94640 | Pressurized or nonpressurized inhalation treatment for acute airway obstruction or for differential diagnosis of pulmonary disease with pharmacologically active bronchodilator; initial | | Covered | "Albuterol nebulization x3 (2.5 mg q20min) administered in office" |

### Hard Stops (Removed from Claim)
None

### Documentation Flags
None

### Payer-Specific Notes
99215 — commercial: Covered. Ref: "The patient's commercial insurance plan covers CPT code 99215 for established patient office visits, particularly when medical decision-making is high complexity, as documented. Modifier 25 is appropriate when a significant, separately identifiable E/M service is performed on the same day as another procedure."
71046 — commercial: Covered. Ref: "Commercial plans typically cover CPT code 71046 for chest X-rays (2 views). Modifier 26 is required when only the professional component (interpretation) is provided, as indicated by the physician's preliminary read."
94640 — commercial: Covered. Ref: "Commercial insurance covers CPT code 94640 for initial bronchodilator inhalation treatments for acute airway obstruction, as documented by the in-office albuterol nebulization."
